document.title: Post-market Surveillance Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Post-market surveillance report for ADD(HAD1417MCW) Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, concluding no adverse benefit-risk effects for the reviewed period.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Summarises post-market surveillance data, benefit-risk assessment, recall checks, complaints, incidents, corrective/preventive actions, and PSUR update period under Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
keyPoints: The report covers Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector model ADD(HAD1417MCW).
The latest listed revision is Revision 4 dated 28 Feb. 2023.
The reviewed time span is February 23, 2021 initial CE marking to December 15, 2022.
FDA, MFDA Korea, and MHRA recall searches found no recalls for the searched terms.
No non-serious incidents, undesirable side-effects, feedbacks, or complaints were identified.
The manufacturer concluded the first PSUR found no adverse benefit-risk effects.
The device risk class is Class IIa and the listed PSUR update period is once in two years.

evidence.pageTarget: Post-market Surveillance Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector revision history

evidence.claim: Revision 4 changed the name from PSUR to PMS report and newly established PUSR according to MDCG 2022-21.
evidence.evidenceText: The name was modified from PSUR → PMS report, and PUSR was newly established according to the form of MDCG 2022-21.

evidence.claim: The report cites Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Article 86 for PSUR preparation by class IIa, IIb, and III device manufacturers.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturers of class IIa, class IIb, and class III devices shall prepare a periodic safety update report in accordance with the article 86 of the Regulation (EU) 2017/745 (MDR).

evidence.claim: The report states PSURs for class III or implantable devices are submitted through the MDR Article 92 electronic system to the notified body.
evidence.evidenceText: For class III devices or implantable devices, manufacturers shall submit PSURs by means of the electronic system referred to in Article 92 of MDR to the notified body involved in the conformity assessment in accordance with Article 52.

evidence.claim: The report states there was no previously generated PMS Report because this was the first medical device sold to the European market after MDR was implemented.
evidence.evidenceText: This is the first medical device to be sold to the European market after MDR was implemented. Therefore, there is no previously generated PMS Report.

evidence.claim: A PSUR had never been created before this report.
evidence.evidenceText: A PSUR has never been created before.
evidence.pageTarget: Periodic Safety Update Report under EU MDR

evidence.claim: The current PSUR did not impact benefit-risk.
evidence.evidenceText: PSUR have not impacted the ‘benefit risk’.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal is FDA, but the document is primarily framed around Regulation (EU) 2017/745 while also referencing FDA, MFDA Korea, and MHRA recall searches.
Document type wording is mixed: title says Post-market Surveillance Report, while several sections refer to PSUR and the revision note says the name was modified from PSUR to PMS report.
Some extracted text contains formatting/OCR artifacts such as merged words and duplicated authority wording, but the core regulatory facts remain readable.
